ambiguities.aspect: Classification of loan as short-term vs long-term
ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: 'short-term loan' with 'one year' term = current asset (Notes Receivable or Loan Receivable)
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IFRS classifies loans due within 12 months as current assets

ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: 'receivable at the end of each month' = monthly accrual of interest income
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IFRS 9 requires effective interest method; for simple interest at stated rate, monthly accrual is appropriate

ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: Stated amount $2,000,000 is the face value advanced
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IFRS 9 requires initial recognition at fair value; for a market-rate short-term loan, face value approximates fair value

best_attempt.reason: Initial loan disbursement recorded at face value; monthly interest of $7,500 ($2,000,000 × 4.5% ÷ 12) will be recognized each month as a separate entry

output_decision_maker.proceed_reason: All terms are explicitly stated: $2,000,000 principal, one-year term, 4.5% annual interest receivable monthly — no ambiguity in initial recognition entry.
output_decision_maker.overall_final_rationale: The transaction clearly states all necessary information: principal amount, term, interest rate, and payment timing, allowing for unambiguous initial recognition of the loan receivable.
